name: Gravel route of upper Cidacos
description:
The Route starts and ends in Calahorra and runs through the Alto Cidacos region. From Calahorra it gradually gains height to border mountains such as Gatún or Valdelavia. Due to the distance, 91 kilometres, and the accumulated difference in altitude of 1450 positive metres, it is a demanding route, which runs mainly along tracks and paths.

As soon as you leave the urban center of Calahorra, you pass through the Laguna de la Estanca and then you enter a wooded area, between Aleppo pine and stone groves. After crossing the road from Soria to Tafalla in Autol, a long ascent of more than 17 kilometers begins to the Yerga mountains, between holm oak and kermes oak groves. Make sure you carry enough water and provisions in Autol, since a section of more than 40 kilometers awaits you in which you will not pass through any population or source.

After passing very close to the summit of Mount Gatún, you begin the descent towards Vilarroya. The route reaches this town practically with the sole mission of being able to supply you, since you have 20 kilometers ahead of you until Arnedo in which you will not find services. Leaving Vilarroya, you pedal northwest, always with a view of the imposing Isasa rock on the horizon.

After Arnedo, take a stretch of the Cidacos greenway to the castle of Quel. From here and until the end of the route in Calahorra, you cross cultivated fields in a gentle descent, and, shortly before reaching your destination, you pass next to the Perdiguero reservoir.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Calahorra offers a diverse landscape for exploration. The region is characterized by the Cidacos river valley, providing routes that follow waterways and pass through varied terrain. Riders can expect a mix of open agricultural areas, historic town surroundings, and ascents towards higher ground like the Yerga Summit. The trails often feature a combination of gravel paths, rural roads, and natural surfaces, catering to the gravel biking experience. Best gravel bike trails around Calahorra The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel route of upper Cidacos, a 57.5 miles (92.5 km) trail that takes 8 hours 56 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the upper Cidacos valley. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Vilarroya – Yerga Summit loop from Rincón de Soto, a difficult 47.0 miles (75.6 km) path. This route includes a challenging climb to the Yerga Summit, offering expansive views. Local gravel bikers also love the Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra, a 46.9 miles (75.5 km) trail leading through the Cidacos Greenway, often completed in about 5 hours 18 minutes. Gravel biking around Calahorra is defined by river valleys, varied rural landscapes, and ascents to local summits. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more advanced gravel trails near Calahorra. The routes in Calahorra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 5 reviews. More than 100 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Calahorra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of gravel biking trails around Calahorra?
A: Calahorra offers a range of gravel biking experiences. While there are &lt;em&gt;over 12 moderate routes&lt;/em&gt;, you'll also find &lt;em&gt;7 challenging trails&lt;/em&gt; for experienced riders. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050719469/gravel-route-of-upper-cidacos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel route of upper Cidacos&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to its significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel biking options in Calahorra?
A: Yes, the region provides options suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; is a great choice. Greenways are typically flatter and safer, making them ideal for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: What are some of the longer gravel bike routes available near Calahorra?
A: For those seeking longer rides, several routes extend over 45 miles (70 km). The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050719469/gravel-route-of-upper-cidacos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel route of upper Cidacos&lt;/a&gt; is approximately 57.5 miles (92.5 km), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38153359" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Perfect Road Routes – Peñalén Gorge loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; covers about 55.9 miles (89.9 km).

Q: Where can I find routes with significant climbs and expansive views?
A: If you're looking for challenging ascents and rewarding vistas, consider routes that head towards higher ground. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31863468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilarroya – Yerga Summit loop from Rincón de Soto&lt;/a&gt; includes a demanding climb to the Yerga Summit, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any gravel routes that pass through historic areas or natural monuments?
A: Absolutely. The region blends natural beauty with historical interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31863162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town – Autol and its Picuezos loop from Estación de ferrocarril de Calahorra&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the historic center of Calahorra and past the unique natural monument of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on gravel bike trails around Calahorra?
A: The gravel trails around Calahorra are incredibly diverse. You'll ride through open agricultural areas, follow the winding Cidacos river valley, and encounter historic town surroundings. Some routes also feature ascents to local summits, providing varied terrain and scenic changes throughout your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Calahorra?
A: While specific conditions vary, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for gravel biking in Calahorra, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite warm, especially in open agricultural areas, while winters might bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain, impacting trail conditions.

Q: Are there any circular gravel routes around Calahorra?
A: Yes, many of the gravel routes in the Calahorra area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31863468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vilarroya – Yerga Summit loop from Rincón de Soto&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742571" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway – Arnedillo and thermal baths loop from Calahorra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What attractions can I find along or near the gravel bike trails?
A: Beyond the trails themselves, you can explore several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4274184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cidacos Greenway&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic path, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5259467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calahorra Old Town&lt;/a&gt; provides historical charm. You might also encounter natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982899" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perdiguero Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the unique rock formations of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1751438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Autol and its Picuezos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in the Calahorra region?
A: There are over 20 gravel bike trails documented for the Calahorra region, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about Calahorra's trails?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 5 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to agricultural fields and challenging climbs, as well as the well-maintained gravel paths that make for an enjoyable experience.
tips: Excellent place to eat and spend a full day of cultural visits. A labyrinth of streets and humble houses as used by the Romans and Jews who lived here for more than five centuries.

Vilarroya is a small municipality in the Arnedo region, in the province of La Rioja and the autonomous community of La Rioja. It is one of the municipalities with the least population in all of Spain, with only five registered inhabitants.

The depopulation of Vilarroya has been gradual throughout the 20th century, until the nearby coal mines were closed in the mid-1960s, which accelerated the process.

Despite being a victim of depopulation, Vilarroya has the Association of Friends of Vilarroya, which, among other activities, paid for the restoration of the parish church of San Juan Bautista.

The church has a single nave, four sections and a head. The first two sections are covered with edges, and the last two sections with a barrel vault. For its part, the head is covered with tiercerons. The tower, with three sections, was built by José Soriano, a resident of Autol, in 1714.

Turruncún is an abandoned town located in the Sierra de Préjano, in the Rioja Baja region.

The coal mines were the great economic engine of the town during the 20th century, when the municipality reached about 300 inhabitants. When the mines were closed, the town slowly decreased until it was completely abandoned.

The ruined village was annexed to the municipality of Arnedo in 1975. Today you can still see the church and an old school, as well as the walls of some of the houses.

The city of Arnedo is part of Baja Rioja and is located at the foot of the Iberian System. It is a place where you can not only visit its churches, the monastery of Vico, do wine tourism or visit its cave of the hundred pillars, but also visit the area of shops and local shoe factories.

Fortress built in the year 1470 formed by a rectangular keep and three heights.

The castle stands on a hill about 100 meters high, with privileged views of the surroundings, where it was possible to monitor and communicate with other fortresses.

It is intuited that, at first, the space was a Roman settlement that only had a watchtower, but that later, and during the Muslim domination, the castle was built.
